testFiles.reason: Existing tests are for Canvas 2D initScene; must be rewritten for Three.js WebGLRenderer/Scene/Camera bootstrap

tests.description: initScene creates a WebGLRenderer and appends its canvas to the container
tests.approach: Call initScene with a container div, verify container.children includes the renderer's domElement (a canvas). Assert renderer.domElement is an HTMLCanvasElement.

tests.description: initScene returns an object with renderer, scene, and camera properties
tests.approach: Call initScene and destructure the return value. Verify all three properties are defined and are instances of their respective Three.js classes.

tests.description: renderer is sized to window dimensions on creation
tests.approach: Set window.innerWidth=1920 and window.innerHeight=1080 before calling initScene. Verify the renderer's domElement has matching width/height (accounting for pixel ratio clamping via setPixelRatio).

tests.description: camera has correct aspect ratio matching window dimensions
tests.approach: Set known window dimensions, call initScene, check camera.aspect equals innerWidth / innerHeight.

tests.description: camera is positioned at z=5 looking at the origin
tests.approach: Call initScene and verify camera.position.z is 5. Verify camera is looking toward origin (position.x and position.y are 0).

tests.description: scene background is set to black (0x000000)
tests.approach: Call initScene and verify scene.background is a THREE.Color with r=0, g=0, b=0.

tests.description: renderer clear color is black
tests.approach: Call initScene. Use renderer.getClearColor(new THREE.Color()) to read back the clear color and verify it is 0x000000.

testFiles.reason: New module src/visual/placeholder.ts needs test coverage for the placeholder 3D object and lighting setup

tests.description: addPlaceholder adds a mesh to the scene
tests.approach: Create a THREE.Scene, call addPlaceholder(scene). Verify scene.children includes at least one Mesh object.
assertions: scene.children includes an object that is a THREE.Mesh

tests.description: addPlaceholder returns an object with a mesh property
tests.approach: Call addPlaceholder and verify the returned object has a mesh property that is a THREE.Mesh.

tests.description: placeholder mesh uses wireframe material
tests.approach: Call addPlaceholder, access result.mesh.material, verify material.wireframe is true.

tests.description: placeholder mesh uses IcosahedronGeometry
tests.approach: Call addPlaceholder, check result.mesh.geometry.type or instanceof IcosahedronGeometry.

tests.description: addPlaceholder adds lighting to the scene
tests.approach: Create a scene, call addPlaceholder, filter scene.children for Light instances. Expect at least an AmbientLight and a DirectionalLight.

tests.description: scene has exactly the expected number of children after addPlaceholder (mesh + 2 lights)
tests.approach: Start with empty scene, call addPlaceholder, count scene.children.

testFiles.reason: Existing tests are for Canvas 2D resize handler; must be rewritten for Three.js renderer/camera resize logic

tests.description: resize handler updates renderer size on window resize
tests.approach: Create renderer and camera via initScene. Attach resize handler. Change window.innerWidth/innerHeight, dispatch resize event. Verify renderer.domElement dimensions changed to match new window size.
assertions: After resize, renderer.getSize() matches new window dimensions

tests.description: resize handler updates camera aspect ratio on window resize
tests.approach: Attach resize handler. Dispatch resize event with new dimensions. Verify camera.aspect equals new innerWidth / innerHeight.

tests.description: resize handler calls camera.updateProjectionMatrix after aspect change
tests.approach: Spy on camera.updateProjectionMatrix before attaching handler. Dispatch resize event. Verify the spy was called.
assertions: camera.updateProjectionMatrix was called at least once

tests.description: resize handler returns a cleanup function that removes the listener
tests.approach: Attach handler, receive cleanup fn. Call cleanup(). Dispatch another resize event. Verify renderer size did NOT change after cleanup.
assertions: After cleanup + resize event, renderer size remains unchanged

tests.description: resize handler handles rapid sequential resize events
tests.approach: Attach handler. Dispatch three resize events with different dimensions in quick succession. Verify final state matches the last dispatched dimensions.
assertions: renderer size matches last resize dimensions
camera aspect matches last resize aspect ratio

testFiles.reason: Existing tests use Canvas 2D startLoop signature; must be rewritten for Three.js renderer/scene/camera signature while preserving audio/pointer/evolution test coverage

tests.description: loop calls requestAnimationFrame recursively
tests.approach: Mock requestAnimationFrame to count calls and invoke callback up to 3 times. Call startLoop with renderer/scene/camera. Verify callCount > 1.

tests.description: renderer.render(scene, camera) is called each frame
tests.approach: Spy on renderer.render. Run 3 frames via mocked RAF. Verify renderer.render was called with scene and camera arguments at least once per frame.

tests.description: loop runs without error when deps are minimal (pre-async resolution)
tests.approach: Call startLoop with renderer/scene/camera and empty deps. Run 2 frames. Expect no throw.

tests.description: placeholder mesh rotates each frame
tests.approach: Add placeholder to scene, record initial rotation.y. Run several frames. Verify rotation.y has increased.
assertions: mesh.rotation.y after N frames > initial mesh.rotation.y
mesh.rotation.x after N frames > initial mesh.rotation.x

tests.description: delta time is computed correctly between frames
tests.approach: Provide a geometry system spy. Feed RAF callbacks with timestamps 0, 16, 50. Verify delta values passed to geometry draw match differences between timestamps.
assertions: delta for frame at t=50 is close to 50-16=34

tests.description: loop integrates audio analyser data when available
tests.approach: Provide mock analyser pipeline with frequency data filled to 200. Verify pipeline.poll() is called and params.bassEnergy > 0.

tests.description: loop renders scene without audio (zero treble when no pipeline)
tests.approach: Provide getAnalyserPipeline returning null. Run frames. Verify params.trebleEnergy === 0 and params.bassEnergy >= 0 (synthetic fallback).

tests.description: loop integrates pointer state changes
tests.approach: Provide getPointerState returning active pointer with dx/dy > 0. Verify params.pointerDisturbance > 0.

tests.description: elapsed time increases monotonically across frames
tests.approach: Run 10 frames with increasing timestamps. Collect elapsed from FrameState. Verify each value > previous.

tests.description: no localStorage or cookie access during render loop
tests.approach: Spy on Storage.prototype.getItem and document.cookie getter. Run several frames. Verify neither spy was called.

tests.description: Canvas 2D context methods (fillRect, fillStyle) are NOT called — rendering uses renderer.render only
tests.approach: Run frames and verify no Canvas 2D draw calls are made. This confirms the migration away from ctx-based clearing/drawing.
assertions: renderer.render is the sole rendering path

testFiles.reason: Verify the updated GeometrySystem interface accepts THREE.Scene instead of CanvasRenderingContext2D — compile-time type check as runtime test

tests.description: GeometrySystem interface accepts THREE.Scene in init and draw methods
tests.approach: Create a mock object satisfying GeometrySystem with init(scene: Scene, ...) and draw(scene: Scene, ...). Verify it can be assigned to a GeometrySystem-typed variable and called without type errors. This is primarily a compile-time check that the type signature was updated.
assertions: init accepts THREE.Scene as first arg
draw accepts THREE.Scene as first arg

changes: Add Three.js WebGLRenderer mock for jsdom environment (jsdom lacks WebGL). Mock should provide: domElement (canvas), setSize(), setPixelRatio(), setClearColor(), getClearColor(), render(), getSize(), dispose(). This allows unit tests to verify renderer interactions without a real WebGL context.

coverageGoals.scene.ts: WebGLRenderer creation, canvas attachment, camera config, scene background, clear color
coverageGoals.placeholder.ts: Mesh creation, geometry type, wireframe material, lighting setup
coverageGoals.resize.ts: Renderer size update, camera aspect update, projection matrix update, cleanup function
coverageGoals.renderLoop.ts: RAF recursion, renderer.render calls, placeholder rotation, audio/pointer integration, delta/elapsed timing, privacy, no Canvas 2D calls
